title: 1-Day Baku Highlights
subtitle: Perfect introduction to Azerbaijan's capital covering Old City, modern landmarks, and local cuisine
bestFor: Short layovers, weekend trips, first-time visitors
overview: Experience the perfect day in Baku, combining ancient history with modern architecture. This itinerary covers the UNESCO-listed Old City, panoramic views from Highland Park, cultural landmarks, and authentic Azerbaijani cuisine.
transportationTips: Use BakuCard for metro and buses (cost: 2 AZN card + 0.3 AZN per ride)
Taxis via Bolt app are affordable (3-7 AZN per trip within city center)
Walking is the best way to explore the Old City (Icherisheher)
Consider a hop-on-hop-off bus for sightseeing (25 AZN for 24-hour ticket)

diningSuggestions.name: Firuze Restaurant
diningSuggestions.highlights: Fresh breakfast platters with cheese, honey, bread, and tea

diningSuggestions.name: Şirvanşah Restaurant
diningSuggestions.highlights: Authentic dolma, plov, and kebabs in historic setting

diningSuggestions.name: Sehrli Təndir
diningSuggestions.highlights: Tandoor-baked bread and meats, cozy atmosphere

hourlySchedule.activity: Start at Icherisheher (Old City) Main Gate
hourlySchedule.description: Enter through the Double Gates and get oriented with the medieval fortress walls.
Climb the 12th-century tower for panoramic views of Baku and the Caspian Sea. Visit the museum inside to learn about its history.
Explore this 15th-century palace complex, a UNESCO World Heritage site. Includes the Divankhana, burial vaults, and mosque.
Stroll through narrow cobblestone streets, visit carpet shops, art galleries, and see historic mosques like Mohammed Mosque.
Enjoy authentic Azerbaijani cuisine at Şirvanşah Restaurant or similar in the Old City.
Walk along the scenic seaside boulevard. Visit the Azerbaijan Carpet Museum (shaped like a rolled carpet) to see traditional carpet weaving.
hourlySchedule.transportation: 15-minute walk from Old City or short taxi ride

hourlySchedule.description: Take a gondola ride through Little Venice canals (seasonal) or enjoy the Ferris wheel for elevated views.
Taxi to the iconic architectural masterpiece designed by Zaha Hadid. Explore the exterior and interior exhibitions (if time allows).
Take a taxi to Highland Park for the best panoramic view of Baku, especially the Flame Towers. Visit Martyrs' Lane memorial.
Watch the sunset and see the Flame Towers light up with LED displays (after dark).
Head to Fountain Square area for dinner at Sehrli Təndir or other local restaurants. Enjoy the lively atmosphere.
hourlySchedule.transportation: Taxi (10 minutes, ~4 AZN) or funicular down

hourlySchedule.description: Walk around the illuminated fountains, enjoy street performances, and maybe try local ice cream.

additionalTips: Wear comfortable walking shoes as you'll cover 8-10 km on foot.
Carry a hat and sunscreen in summer; the Old City has limited shade.
Book tickets online for Maiden Tower and Shirvanshahs Palace to skip lines.
Bargain politely at souvenir shops in the Old City (start at 50% of asking price).
Have some local currency (AZN) as some small vendors don't accept cards.
Download offline maps (Google Maps or Maps.me) as Old City streets can be confusing.
